raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Approximately 78% of California’s active registered voters received a ballot in the mail before the March Primary Election this year.",
    "At least 16 states either postponed their scheduled primary elections or switched them to vote by mail elections due to concerns about conducting in-person voting during the spread of COVID-19.",
    "Mailing a ballot to every California voter provides the opportunity to vote from the safety of their own home.",
    "The passage of AB 860 proactively ensures that active registered voters in California will receive a ballot in the mail.",
    "Senate Bill 423 requires county elections officials to establish and maintain a minimum number of polling places and vote centers for the statewide general election.",
    "California has been sending mail-in ballots to voters safely and securely for decades."
  ]
}
```
